meaning: n.希腊产的饭后酒之一种
examples: IN GREECE ouzo and olives have given way to debt and downgrades.
When I was younger, when I was a teenager I drank this Greek liqueur, ouzo, with beer.
Call this the grappa or ouzo variety: locals swear by it, outsiders balk at it, and too much can be fatal.
The Groupon Web page for first-time users features an image of two cocktails, and its ad copy often touts that drinks like margaritas, ouzo or beer are included in the deal.
All those fine young men and women at Goldman Sachs, JP Morgan, Morgan Stanley, Merrill Lynch, and UBS might just as well take a three Martini or maybe three Ouzo lunch for the next couple of days.
